リンク先変更
[[府中市 (東京都)]]
+同名
改名提案
改名先変更
アルビレオ がページ「[[大國魂神社]]」を「[[大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]」に移動しました: [[ノート:大國魂神社 (府中市)]]の議論により、府中市の神社を代表項目とするため
改名の反映と、同名社の追加
revision.text:
'''大国魂神社/大國魂神社'''（おおくにたまじんじゃ）は[[神社]]。
* [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)]]（[[磐城国]]） - [[福島県]][[いわき市]]鎮座。 * [[大國魂神社 (府中市)]]（[[武蔵国]]） - [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]鎮座。武蔵国の[[総社]]。（[[くらやみ祭]]、[[酉の市]]）
* 読みが同じ神社 ** [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)]] - [[茨城県]][[桜川市]]
* 同じ読みの前に地名が付く神社 ** [[大和大国魂神社]] - [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]] ** [[尾張大国霊神社]] - [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]]
{{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおくにたましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

revision.comment: 府中市の神社の改名により修正

* [[大國魂神社]]（[[武蔵国]]） - [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]鎮座。武蔵国の[[総社]]。（[[くらやみ祭]]、[[酉の市]]） * [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)]]（[[磐城国]]） - [[福島県]][[いわき市]]鎮座。
* 読みが同じ神社 ** [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)]] - [[茨城県]][[桜川市]]
* 同じ読みの前に地名が付く神社 ** [[大和大国魂神社]] - [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]] ** [[尾張大国霊神社]] - [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]]
{{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおくにたましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

revision.comment: 追加
revision.text:
'''大国魂神社/大國魂神社'''（おおくにたまじんじゃ）は[[神社]]。
* [[大國魂神社]]（[[武蔵国]]） - [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]鎮座。武蔵国の[[総社]]。（[[くらやみ祭]]、[[酉の市]]） * [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)]]（[[磐城国]]） - [[福島県]][[いわき市]]鎮座。 * 大国魂神社（大黒さん）- [[堺市]][[堺区]] [[開口神社]]の境内外社。
* 読みが同じ神社 ** [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)]] - [[茨城県]][[桜川市]]
* 同じ読みの前に地名が付く神社 ** [[大和大国魂神社]] - [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]] ** [[尾張大国霊神社]] - [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]] ** 島大国魂神社 - [[長崎県]][[対馬市]]。⇒[[対馬国の式内社一覧]] ** 会津大国魂神社 - [[福島県]][[会津美里町]]。[[伊佐須美神社]]の境内外社。
{{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおくにたましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

revision.comment: 大国玉神社の追加
revision.text:
'''大国魂神社/大國魂神社'''（おおくにたまじんじゃ）は[[神社]]。
* [[大國魂神社]]（[[武蔵国]]） - [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]鎮座。武蔵国の[[総社]]。（[[くらやみ祭]]、[[酉の市]]） * [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)]]（[[磐城国]]） - [[福島県]][[いわき市]]鎮座。 * 大国魂神社（大黒さん）- [[堺市]][[堺区]] [[開口神社]]の境内外社。
* 読みが同じ神社 ** [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)]] - [[茨城県]][[桜川市]] ** 大国玉神社 - [[三重県]][[多気郡]]。⇒[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] ** 大国玉神社 - [[長崎県]][[壱岐郡]]。⇒[[壱岐国の式内社一覧]]
* 同じ読みの前に地名が付く神社 ** [[大和大国魂神社]] - [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]] ** [[尾張大国霊神社]] - [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]] ** 島大国魂神社 - [[長崎県]][[対馬市]]。⇒[[対馬国の式内社一覧]] ** 会津大国魂神社 - [[福島県]][[会津美里町]]。[[伊佐須美神社]]の境内外社。
{{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおくにたましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

* [[大國魂神社]]（[[武蔵国]]） - [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]。武蔵国の[[総社]]（[[くらやみ祭]]、[[酉の市]]） * [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)]]（[[磐城国]]） - [[福島県]][[いわき市]] * 大国魂神社（大黒さん） - [[堺市]][[堺区]]。[[開口神社]]の境内外社 * 読みが同じ神社 ** [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)]] - [[茨城県]][[桜川市]] ** 大国玉神社 - [[三重県]][[多気郡]]⇒[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] ** 大国玉神社 - [[長崎県]][[壱岐郡]]⇒[[壱岐島の式内社一覧]] * 同じ読みの前に地名が付く神社 ** [[倭大国魂神社]] - [[徳島県]][[美馬市]] ** [[大和大国魂神社]] - [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]] ** [[尾張大国霊神社]] - [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]] ** 島大国魂神社 - [[長崎県]][[対馬市]]⇒[[対馬島の式内社一覧]] ** 会津大国魂神社 - [[福島県]][[会津美里町]]。[[伊佐須美神社]]の境内外社

revision.text:
'''大国魂神社・大國魂神社'''（おおくにたまじんじゃ）は、[[神社]]。
== 主な大国魂神社 == * [[会津大国魂神社]] – [[福島県]][[大沼郡]][[会津美里町]] ： [[伊佐須美神社]]境内社。 * [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)|大國魂神社]] – [[福島県]][[いわき市]]平菅波宮前 * [[大國魂神社]] – [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[宮町 (東京都府中市)|宮町]] ： [[武蔵国]][[総社]]、[[くらやみ祭]]（例大祭）。 * [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)|大国玉神社]] – [[茨城県]][[桜川市]]大国玉 * [[尾張大国霊神社]] – [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]][[国府宮 (稲沢市)|国府宮]] ： 国府宮神社。 * [[大国玉神社 (松阪市)|大国玉神社]] – [[三重県]][[松阪市]]六根町 * [[大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|大国魂神社]] – [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]][[甲斐町東]] ： [[開口神社]]の境内社。大黒さん。 * [[大和大国魂神社]] – [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]]榎列上幡多 * [[倭大国魂神社]] – [[徳島県]][[美馬市]][[美馬町]] * [[大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|大國玉神社]] – [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]郷ノ浦 * [[島大國魂神社]] – 長崎県[[対馬市]]上対馬町
== 関連項目 ==
* [[国魂]] {{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおくにたましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

revision.text:
'''大国魂神社・大國魂神社'''（おおくにたまじんじゃ）は、「大国魂」「大國魂」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。
== 主な大国魂神社 == * [[会津大国魂神社]] – [[福島県]][[大沼郡]][[会津美里町]] ： [[伊佐須美神社]]境内社。 * [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)|大國魂神社]] – [[福島県]][[いわき市]]平菅波宮前 * [[大國魂神社]] – [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[宮町 (東京都府中市)|宮町]] ： [[武蔵国]][[総社]]、[[くらやみ祭]]（例大祭）。 * [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)|大国玉神社]] – [[茨城県]][[桜川市]]大国玉 * [[尾張大国霊神社]] – [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]][[国府宮 (稲沢市)|国府宮]] ： 国府宮神社。 * [[大国玉神社 (松阪市)|大国玉神社]] – [[三重県]][[松阪市]]六根町 * [[大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|大国魂神社]] – [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]][[甲斐町東]] ： [[開口神社]]の境内社。大黒さん。 * [[大和大国魂神社]] – [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]]榎列上幡多 * [[倭大国魂神社]] – [[徳島県]][[美馬市]][[美馬町]] * [[大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|大國玉神社]] – [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]郷ノ浦 * [[島大國魂神社]] – 長崎県[[対馬市]]上対馬町
== 関連項目 ==
* [[国魂]] {{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおくにたましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

revision.text:
'''大国魂神社・大國魂神社'''（おおくにたまじんじゃ）は、｢大国魂」｢大國魂」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。｢大国霊」｢大国玉」とも表記する。
== 主な大国魂神社 ==
=== 大国魂神社 === * [[会津大国魂神社]] – [[福島県]][[大沼郡]][[会津美里町]]宮林 ： [[伊佐須美神社]]境内社 * [[大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|大国魂神社]] – [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]][[甲斐町東]] ： [[開口神社]]境内社 * [[大和大国魂神社]] – [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]]榎列 * [[倭大国魂神社]] – [[徳島県]][[美馬市]][[美馬町]]重清
=== 大國魂神社 ===
* [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)|大國魂神社]] – [[福島県]][[いわき市]]平菅波 * [[大國魂神社]] – [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[宮町 (東京都府中市)|宮町]] ： [[武蔵国]][[総社]]、[[くらやみ祭]] (例大祭) * [[島大國魂神社]] – 長崎県[[対馬市]]上対馬町
=== 大国霊神社 ===
* [[尾張大国霊神社]] – [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]][[国府宮 (稲沢市)|国府宮]] ： 国府宮神社
=== 大国玉神社 ===
* [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)|大国玉神社]] – [[茨城県]][[桜川市]]大国玉 ： 大國玉神社 * [[大国玉神社 (松阪市)|大国玉神社]] – [[三重県]][[松阪市]]六根町 * [[大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|大國玉神社]] – [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]郷ノ浦町
== 関連項目 ==
* [[国魂]] {{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおくにたましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

'''大国魂神社/大國魂神社'''（おおくにたまじんじゃ）は[[神社]]。
* [[大國魂神社]]（[[武蔵国]]） - [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]]。武蔵国の[[総社]]（[[くらやみ祭]]、[[酉の市]]） * [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)]]（[[磐城国]]） - [[福島県]][[いわき市]] * 大国魂神社（大黒さん） - [[堺市]][[堺区]]。[[開口神社]]の境内外社 * 読みが同じ神社 ** [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)]] - [[茨城県]][[桜川市]] ** 大国玉神社 - [[三重県]][[多気郡]]⇒[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] ** 大国玉神社 - [[長崎県]][[壱岐郡]]⇒[[壱岐島の式内社一覧]] * 同じ読みの前に地名が付く神社 ** [[倭大国魂神社]] - [[徳島県]][[美馬市]] ** [[大和大国魂神社]] - [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]] ** [[尾張大国霊神社]] - [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]] ** 島大国魂神社 - [[長崎県]][[対馬市]]⇒[[対馬島の式内社一覧]] ** 会津大国魂神社 - [[福島県]][[会津美里町]]。[[伊佐須美神社]]の境内外社

'''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (大国魂神社/大國魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}.
* {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Musashi Province)|ja|大國魂神社}} ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}) - Located in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}. It is the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of Musashi Province and hosts the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}, and {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki City)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki Province|ja|磐城国}}) - Located in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine (Daikoku-san) - Located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市堺区}}, an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Kaikō Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
== Shrines with the same pronunciation == * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Sakuragawa City)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} - Located in {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|ja|桜川市}}. * Ōkunidama Shrine - Located in {{ill|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}. * Ōkunidama Shrine - Located in {{ill|Iki District, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐郡}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の式内社一覧}}.
== Shrines with a location prefix == * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|倭大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|ja|美馬市}}. * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|大和大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo|ja|南あわじ市}}. * {{ill|Owari Ōkunidama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} - Located in {{ill|Inazawa, Aichi|ja|稲沢市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine of Shima - Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|ja|対馬市}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Tsushima Island|ja|対馬島の式内社一覧}}. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Aizumisato, Fukushima|ja|会津美里町}}, an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}.
{{Disambiguation}}

It is the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of Musashi Province and hosts the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}, and {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki City)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki Province|ja|磐城国}}) - Located in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine (Daikoku-san) - Located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市堺区}}, an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Kaikō Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
== Shrines with the same pronunciation == * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Sakuragawa City)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} - Located in {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|ja|桜川市}}. * Ōkunidama Shrine - Located in {{ill|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}. * Ōkunidama Shrine - Located in {{ill|Iki District, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐郡}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の式内社一覧}}.
== Shrines with a location prefix == * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|倭大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|ja|美馬市}}. * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|大和大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo|ja|南あわじ市}}. * {{ill|Owari Ōkunidama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} - Located in {{ill|Inazawa, Aichi|ja|稲沢市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine of Shima - Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|ja|対馬市}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Tsushima Island|ja|対馬島の式内社一覧}}. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Aizumisato, Fukushima|ja|会津美里町}}, an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}.

'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}. * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunirei Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitsu|ja|国魂}}

== Shrines with a location prefix == * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|倭大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|ja|美馬市}}. * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|大和大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo|ja|南あわじ市}}. * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} - Located in {{ill|Inazawa, Aichi|ja|稲沢市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine of Shima - Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|ja|対馬市}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Tsushima Island|ja|対馬島の式内社一覧}}. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Aizumisato, Fukushima|ja|会津美里町}}, an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}.

'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}. * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunirei Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitsu|ja|国魂}}

'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunidama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitsu|ja|国魂}}

'''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (大国魂神社/大國魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}.
* {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Musashi Province)|ja|大國魂神社}} ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}) - Located in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}. It is the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of Musashi Province and hosts the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}, and {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki City)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki Province|ja|磐城国}}) - Located in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine (Daikoku-san) - Located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市堺区}}, an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Kaikō Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
== Shrines with the same pronunciation == * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa City)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} - Located in {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|ja|桜川市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine - Located in {{ill|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine - Located in {{ill|Iki District, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐郡}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の式内社一覧}}.
== Shrines with a location prefix == * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|倭大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|ja|美馬市}}. * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|大和大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo|ja|南あわじ市}}. * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} - Located in {{ill|Inazawa, Aichi|ja|稲沢市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine of Shima - Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|ja|対馬市}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Tsushima Island|ja|対馬島の式内社一覧}}. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Aizumisato, Fukushima|ja|会津美里町}}, an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}.

== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.

* {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Musashi Province)|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}) - Located in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}. It is the {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社}} of Musashi Province and hosts the {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}, and {{ill|Tori no Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki City)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} ({{ill|Iwaki Province|ja|磐城国}}) - Located in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine (Daikoku-san) - Located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市堺区}}, an auxiliary shrine within {{ill|Kaikō Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
== Shrines with the same pronunciation == * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa City)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} - Located in {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|ja|桜川市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine - Located in {{ill|Taki District, Mie|ja|多気郡}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine - Located in {{ill|Iki District, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐郡}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の式内社一覧}}.
== Shrines with a location prefix == * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|倭大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|ja|美馬市}}. * {{ill|Yamato Ōkunitama Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|大和大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo|ja|南あわじ市}}. * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} - Located in {{ill|Inazawa, Aichi|ja|稲沢市}}. * Ōkunitama Shrine of Shima - Located in {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|ja|対馬市}}, see {{ill|List of Shikinai Shrines in Tsushima Island|ja|対馬島の式内社一覧}}. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} - Located in {{ill|Aizumisato, Fukushima|ja|会津美里町}}, an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}.

'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.

'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.

'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.

'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.

== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.

[[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[sn:Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)]][[sn:Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)]]'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

[[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[sn:Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)]][[sn:Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)]]'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

[[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]
[[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[sn:Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)]][[sn:Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)]]'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

[[Category:Shrine disambiguations]]
[[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[category:kk]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]][[Category:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[sn:Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)]][[sn:Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)]]'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|国|ja|国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|ja|大沼郡}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|会津美里町}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|ja|伊佐須美神社}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Sakai Ward|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji City|ja|南あわじ市}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Mima City|ja|美馬市}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Iwaki City|ja|いわき市}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|大國魂神社|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Fuchū City|ja|府中市 (東京都)}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|対馬市}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|尾張大国霊神社}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, {{ill|Inazawa City|ja|稲沢市}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa City|ja|桜川市}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Matsusaka City|ja|松阪市}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}}

revision.text:
'''大国魂神社・大國魂神社'''（おおくにたまじんじゃ）は、｢大国魂」｢大國魂」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。｢大国霊」｢大国玉」とも表記する。
== 主な大国魂神社 ==
=== 大国魂神社 === * [[会津大国魂神社]] – [[福島県]][[大沼郡]][[会津美里町]]宮林 ： [[伊佐須美神社]]境内社 * [[大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|大国魂神社]] – [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]][[甲斐町東]] ： [[開口神社]]境内社 * [[大和大国魂神社]] – [[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]]榎列 * [[倭大国魂神社]] – [[徳島県]][[美馬市]][[美馬町]]重清
=== 大國魂神社 ===
* [[大國魂神社 (いわき市)|大國魂神社]] – [[福島県]][[いわき市]]平菅波 * [[大國魂神社]] – [[東京都]][[府中市 (東京都)|府中市]][[宮町 (東京都府中市)|宮町]] ： [[武蔵国]][[総社]]、[[くらやみ祭]] (例大祭) * [[島大國魂神社]] – 長崎県[[対馬市]]上対馬町
=== 大国霊神社 ===
* [[尾張大国霊神社]] – [[愛知県]][[稲沢市]][[国府宮 (稲沢市)|国府宮]] ： 国府宮神社
=== 大国玉神社 ===
* [[大国玉神社 (桜川市)|大国玉神社]] – [[茨城県]][[桜川市]]大国玉 ： 大國玉神社 * [[大国玉神社 (松阪市)|大国玉神社]] – [[三重県]][[松阪市]]六根町 * [[大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|大國玉神社]] – [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]郷ノ浦町
== 関連項目 ==
* [[国魂]] {{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:おおくにたましんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

revision.text:
'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character [[:en:Country|国]] in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|qq=}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōnuma District|Ōnuma District]], {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato Town|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|qq=}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]]. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]], [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai Ward]], {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaimachi-higashi|qq=}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikou Shrine|qq=}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji City]], Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima City]], {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimamachi|qq=}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|qq=}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]], Hirasuganami. * [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Fuchū, Tokyo|Fuchū City]], {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|qq=}}: the head shrine (総社) of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima City]], Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|qq=}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|qq=}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * [[:en:Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Inazawa|Inazawa City]], {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|qq=}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|qq=}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|Sakuragawa City]], Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka City]], Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama]]

Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character [[:en:Country|国]] in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|qq=}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōnuma District|Ōnuma District]], {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato Town|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|qq=}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]]. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]], [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai Ward]], {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaimachi-higashi|qq=}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikou Shrine|qq=}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji City]], Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima City]], {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimamachi|qq=}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|qq=}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]], Hirasuganami. * [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Fuchū, Tokyo|Fuchū City]], {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|qq=}}: the head shrine (総社) of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima City]], Kamitsushima Town.

The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * [[:en:Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Inazawa|Inazawa City]], {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|qq=}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|qq=}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|Sakuragawa City]], Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka City]], Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Gounoura Town.

== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|qq=}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]], Hirasuganami. * [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Fuchū, Tokyo|Fuchū City]], {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|qq=}}: the head shrine (総社) of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima City]], Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|qq=}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|qq=}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * [[:en:Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Inazawa|Inazawa City]], {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|qq=}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|qq=}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|Sakuragawa City]], Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka City]], Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Gounoura Town.

revision.text:
'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character [[:en:Country|国]] in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|qq=}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōnuma District|Ōnuma District]], {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato Town|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|qq=}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]]. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]], [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai Ward]], {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaimachi-higashi|qq=}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikou Shrine|qq=}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji City]], Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima City]], {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimamachi|qq=}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|qq=}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]], Hirasuganami. * [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Fuchū, Tokyo|Fuchū City]], {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|qq=}}: the head shrine (総社) of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima City]], Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|qq=}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|qq=}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * [[:en:Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Inazawa|Inazawa City]], {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|qq=}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|qq=}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|Sakuragawa City]], Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka City]], Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama]]

The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * [[:en:Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Inazawa|Inazawa City]], {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|qq=}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.

'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
