revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Has wikidata]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433669]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character [[:en:Country|国]] in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōnuma District|Ōnuma District]], {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato Town|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of [[:en:Isasumi Shrine|Isasumi Shrine]]. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai City]], [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai Ward]], {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaimachi-higashi|1=Kaimachi-higashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912534}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikou Shrine|1=Aguchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655187}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji City]], Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|qq=}} – located in [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Mima, Tokushima|Mima City]], {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimamachi|1=Mimachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609479}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|1=Ōkunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433670}} – located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]], Hirasuganami. * [[:en:Ōkunitama Shrine|Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Fuchū, Tokyo|Fuchū City]], {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|1=Miyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116980587}}: the head shrine (総社) of [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Tsushima, Nagasaki|Tsushima City]], Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * [[:en:Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine]] – located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Inazawa|Inazawa City]], {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|1=Kōnomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126306347}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|1=玉|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572333}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|1=Okunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433665}} – located in [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|Sakuragawa City]], Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka City]], Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama]]

== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q11465296|lt=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inazawa|en|Inazawa|WD=Q389562|lt=Inazawa City}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|1=Kōnomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126306347}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.

It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|1=Okunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433665}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|en|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|WD=Q990350|lt=Sakuragawa City}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka City}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}}, Gounoura Town.

The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q11465296|lt=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inazawa|en|Inazawa|WD=Q389562|lt=Inazawa City}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|1=Kōnomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126306347}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.

It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|1=Okunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433665}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|en|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|WD=Q990350|lt=Sakuragawa City}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka City}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}}, Gounoura Town.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11433669]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|Country|en|Country|WD=Q6256|lt=国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|en|Ōnuma District|WD=Q1155853|lt=Ōnuma District}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato Town|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai City}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai Ward}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaimachi-higashi|1=Kaimachi-higashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912534}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikou Shrine|1=Aguchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655187}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji|en|Minamiawaji|WD=Q869004|lt=Minamiawaji City}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|en|Mima, Tokushima|WD=Q857828|lt=Mima City}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimamachi|1=Mimachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609479}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|1=Ōkunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433670}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q611678|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|en|Fuchū, Tokyo|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū City}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|1=Miyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116980587}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima City}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q11465296|lt=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inazawa|en|Inazawa|WD=Q389562|lt=Inazawa City}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|1=Kōnomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126306347}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|Country|en|Country|WD=Q6256|lt=国}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|en|Ōnuma District|WD=Q1155853|lt=Ōnuma District}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato Town|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai City}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai Ward}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaimachi-higashi|1=Kaimachi-higashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912534}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikou Shrine|1=Aguchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655187}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji|en|Minamiawaji|WD=Q869004|lt=Minamiawaji City}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|en|Mima, Tokushima|WD=Q857828|lt=Mima City}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimamachi|1=Mimachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609479}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|1=Ōkunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433670}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q611678|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|en|Fuchū, Tokyo|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū City}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|1=Miyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116980587}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima City}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q11465296|lt=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inazawa|en|Inazawa|WD=Q389562|lt=Inazawa City}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|1=Kōnomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126306347}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|1=玉|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572333}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|1=Okunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433665}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|en|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|WD=Q990350|lt=Sakuragawa City}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka City}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|en|Kunitama|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama}}

== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=Country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|1=Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433670}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q611678|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|en|Fuchū, Tokyo|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū City|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|1=Miyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116980587}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima City|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=Soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=Soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q11465296|lt=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|1=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inazawa|en|Inazawa|WD=Q389562|lt=Inazawa City|1=Inazawa}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|1=Kōnomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126306347}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|1=Q11572333|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572333}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|1=Okunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433665}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|en|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|WD=Q990350|lt=Sakuragawa City|1=Sakuragawa, Ibaraki}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka City|1=Matsusaka}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Gounoura Town.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大国魂神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
'''Ōkunitama Shrine / Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is the name used for certain Shinto shrines, rendered either as "大国魂神社" or "大國魂神社", and also sometimes written as "大国霊神社" or "大国玉神社". Below is an explanation of the differences in the kanji spellings followed by a list of the major shrines under each spelling.
== Spelled as "大国魂神社" == This form uses the simplified character {{ill|Country|en|Country|WD=Q6256|lt=国|1=Country}} in place of the traditional {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=Country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}}. It is the form commonly used in modern contexts. * {{ill|Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|会津大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōnuma District|en|Ōnuma District|WD=Q1155853|lt=Ōnuma District|1=Ōnuma District}}, {{ill|Aizumisato Town|ja|Aizumisato|1=Aizumisato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato Town|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizumisato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Miyabayashi: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Isasumi Shrine|en|Isasumi Shrine|WD=Q3155136|lt=Isasumi Shrine|1=Isasumi Shrine}}. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja|大国魂神社 (堺市堺区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakai City, Sakai Ward)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai City|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai Ward|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Kaimachi-higashi|ja|甲斐町東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaimachi-higashi|1=Q131912534|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912534}}: a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kaikou Shrine|ja|開口神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaikou Shrine|1=Aguchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655187}}.
=== Branches of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] === * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|ja|大和大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Minamiawaji|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minamiawaji|en|Minamiawaji|WD=Q869004|lt=Minamiawaji City|1=Minamiawaji}}, Enokiretsu. * {{ill|Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|ja|倭大国魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine, Mima|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mima, Tokushima|en|Mima, Tokushima|WD=Q857828|lt=Mima City|1=Mima, Tokushima}}, {{ill|Mimamachi|ja|美馬町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimamachi|1=Mimachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609479}}, Shigekiyo.
== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=Country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|1=Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433670}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q611678|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|en|Fuchū, Tokyo|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū City|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|1=Miyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116980587}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima City|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=Soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=Soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q11465296|lt=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|1=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inazawa|en|Inazawa|WD=Q389562|lt=Inazawa City|1=Inazawa}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|1=Kōnomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126306347}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|1=Q11572333|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572333}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|1=Okunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433665}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|en|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|WD=Q990350|lt=Sakuragawa City|1=Sakuragawa, Ibaraki}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka City|1=Matsusaka}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Gounoura Town.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kunitama|en|Kunitama|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama|1=Kunitama}}

== Spelled as "大國魂神社" == This form retains the traditional character {{ill|國|ja|國|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/國|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国|1=Country|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6256}} and is often used by shrines that emphasize historical prestige or formality. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|大國魂神社 (いわき市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|1=Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433670}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Hirasuganami. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q611678|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine|1=Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|en|Fuchū, Tokyo|WD=Q208818|lt=Fuchū City|1=Fuchū, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Miyamachi|ja|宮町 (東京都府中市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamachi|1=Miyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116980587}}: the head shrine (総社) of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, hosting the annual grand festival {{ill|Kurayami Festival|ja|くらやみ祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayami Festival|1=Kurayami Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11265601}}. * {{ill|Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja|島大國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Ōkunitama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tsushima, Nagasaki|en|Tsushima, Nagasaki|WD=Q991032|lt=Tsushima City|1=Tsushima, Nagasaki}}, Kamitsushima Town.
== Spelled as "大国霊神社" == This variant uses the character {{ill|霊|ja|霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/霊|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=Soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (rei) instead of {{ill|魂|ja|魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/魂|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霊魂|1=Soul|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9165}} (tama). The reading may be "Ōkunitama Jinja" or "Ōkunirei Jinja". * {{ill|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|en|Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|WD=Q11465296|lt=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine|1=Owari Ōkunitama Shrine}} – located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inazawa|en|Inazawa|WD=Q389562|lt=Inazawa City|1=Inazawa}}, {{ill|Kokufumiya Shrine|ja|国府宮 (稲沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufumiya Shrine|1=Kōnomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126306347}}: also known as Kokufumiya Shrine.
== Spelled as "大国玉神社" == This variant employs the character {{ill|玉|ja|玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/玉|1=Q11572333|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572333}} (tama) to denote sacred treasures. It is usually rendered in the simplified form "大国玉神社", though in some cases the traditional form "大國玉神社" is used. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|ja|大国玉神社 (桜川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Sakuragawa)|1=Okunitama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433665}} – located in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|en|Sakuragawa, Ibaraki|WD=Q990350|lt=Sakuragawa City|1=Sakuragawa, Ibaraki}}, Ogunitama: also referred to as 大國玉神社. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja|大国玉神社 (松阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Matsusaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka City|1=Matsusaka}}, Rokkon Town. * {{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja|大國玉神社 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunitama Shrine (Iki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – located in {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Gounoura Town.
